title: MAW v6 Handoff Schemas
description: Structured handoff payloads for the Multi-Agent Workflow v6 pipeline. Each schema defines the contract between two agents (or an agent and an external system) at a specific transition point in the PM -> Builder -> Tester -> TPM pipeline.

issue_ref.description: Minimal reference to a Linear issue.

url.description: Direct URL to the Linear issue.

t_shirt_size.description: T-shirt size estimate. Maps to Linear points: XS=1, S=2, M=3, L=5, XL=8.

deployment_path.description: 'default' targets agentdash-main directly. 'staging-required' targets the staging branch first (XL issues with high-risk surface area).

label_set.description: Current Linear labels on the issue at the time of handoff.

cuj_entry.description: A single Critical User Journey reference.

test_result_entry.description: Result of a single test or CUJ verification.

detail.description: Failure message, skip reason, or notes.

evidence_path.description: Path or URL to screenshot, GIF, or log artifact.

code_review_finding.description: A single code review finding from the Tester's diff review.

file.description: Relative file path in the repository.

line.description: Line number of the finding (approximate is acceptable).

severity.description: Severity level. Critical/High block the handoff; Medium/Low are informational.

description.description: What the issue is and why it matters.

regression_gate_result.description: Result of the mandatory project-wide regression suite (typecheck + test + build).

test_fail_count.description: Number of failing test cases (0 when test is 'pass').

pre_existing_failures.description: Names of test cases that failed but are known pre-existing flakes unrelated to this change.

pm_to_builder.title: PM -> Builder Handoff
pm_to_builder.description: Payload the PM agent produces when an issue is fully elaborated and ready for implementation. Posted as a Linear comment and consumed by the Builder agent (or the /workon orchestrator).

estimate_points.description: Linear estimate points corresponding to the size.

pr_target_branch.description: Branch the PR should target. 'main' for default path, 'staging' for staging-required.

acceptance_criteria.description: Ordered list of acceptance criteria. Each item is a testable statement.

cujs.description: Critical User Journeys affected by this issue. Required for M+ sizes.

test_plan.description: Test plan for the Tester agent. Required for M+ sizes.

automated_command.description: Exact shell command to run the automated test suite, e.g. 'pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/billing/checkout.spec.ts'.

manual_checks.description: Manual verification steps that require human or browser-driven checking.

out_of_scope.description: Explicitly excluded items to prevent scope creep.

user_stories.description: User stories in 'As a <role>, I want <goal>, so that <benefit>' format.

staging_required.description: True when the XL issue needs staging validation before production.

builder_to_ci.title: Builder -> CI / Tester Handoff
builder_to_ci.description: Payload the Builder agent produces after implementation is complete and the PR is ready for testing. Posted as a Linear comment with the PR-Ready label. Consumed by the Tester agent.

epic.description: Epic label inherited from PM handoff.

url.description: Direct URL to the pull request.

commit_count.description: Number of commits in the PR.

branch.description: Feature branch name (shorthand, same as pr.head_branch).

e2e_tests.description: E2E test files created or modified by Builder (S+ sizes).

files.description: Relative paths to E2E test files.

run_command.description: Command to execute the E2E tests for this feature.

cujs.description: CUJs carried forward from PM handoff for Tester to verify.

execution_engine.description: Which execution engine was used. 'direct' for XS/S, 'omc-team-N' for M/L, 'omc-ralph' for XL.

implementation_notes.description: Free-form notes from Builder about decisions, risks, or areas Tester should focus on.

fix_attempt.description: Number of fix iterations this PR has been through (0 = first submission, 1 = first fix, 2 = second fix). Max 2 before human escalation.

tester_to_reviewer.title: Tester -> Reviewer / Human Handoff
tester_to_reviewer.description: Payload the Tester agent produces after completing automated tests, code review, and Chrome CUJ verification. Contains the full evidence package. Consumed by human reviewers (or auto-verified for XS/S in pre-real-users mode).

verdict.description: 'pass' -> Locally-Tested/Staging-Tested. 'fail' -> Tests-Failed, routes back to Builder. 'blocked' -> environment or infra issue, escalate to human.

quality_gate_label.description: The quality gate label applied when verdict is 'pass'.

total.description: Total number of E2E test cases executed.

ultraqa_cycles.description: Number of UltraQA auto-fix cycles consumed (0-5). Does not count against the manual retry budget.

code_review.description: Code review findings from the diff review phase.

findings.description: All findings, ordered by severity descending.

blocking_count.description: Number of critical + high findings that block the handoff.

cuj_verification.description: Chrome CUJ walk-through results. Populated for all sizes (inline for XS/S, qa-tester delegated for M+).

console_errors.description: JavaScript console errors observed during CUJ verification. Empty array means clean.

network_failures.description: Non-2xx network requests observed during CUJ verification.

human_only_checklist.description: Items that only a human can verify (third-party dashboards, email delivery, subjective content quality). Empty for XS/S issues.

failure_sub_issues.description: Sub-issues created in Linear for each failure (only when verdict is 'fail').

fix_attempt.description: Current retry count. 0 = first pass, 1 = after first Builder fix, 2 = after second. At 2+ with verdict 'fail', escalate to human instead of re-spawning Builder.

reviewer_to_tpm.title: Reviewer -> TPM Handoff
reviewer_to_tpm.description: Payload representing a human-verified (or auto-verified) issue ready for TPM to merge and deploy. The TPM agent reads this from Linear labels/comments during '/tpm sync'.

pr.description: The PR to merge. For staging-required issues, this may be PR #2 (feature -> main) which the TPM creates.

url.description: Direct URL to the pull request.

base_branch.description: Must be 'main' for TPM to merge. Staging-required issues get a second PR.

is_production_pr.description: True when this is PR #2 created by TPM for a staging-required issue.

verification_method.description: How the Human-Verified label was applied. 'human' = a person reviewed. 'auto-xs-s' = XS/S auto-ship rule. 'auto-pre-real-users' = pre-real-users mode (all sizes auto-verified).

human_checklist_items_verified.description: Which human-only checklist items were explicitly verified. Empty for auto-verified issues.

wave.description: Wave context if this issue is part of a multi-wave project.

completed_in_wave.description: Issues already shipped in this wave (before this one).

tpm_merge_report.title: TPM Merge Report
tpm_merge_report.description: Payload the TPM agent produces after completing the merge-deploy-smoke cycle for one issue. Posted as a Linear comment and used for dashboard reporting. This is the terminal handoff -- the issue moves to 'Done' state.

merge_result.description: 'shipped' = merged + smoke passed + In-Production. 'reverted' = merged but smoke failed, auto-reverted. 'blocked' = could not merge (conflicts, CI failure, etc.).

pr.description: The PR that was merged (or attempted).

merge_sha.description: Merge commit SHA on the target branch. Null if merge failed.

revert_sha.description: Revert commit SHA if smoke tests failed and the merge was reverted.

frontend_status.description: HTTP status code from the production frontend URL.

command.description: The smoke test command that was run.

staging_rebase.description: Staging branch rebase result (only for staging-required issues).

performed.description: Whether staging was rebased onto main after this merge.

success.description: Whether the rebase completed without conflicts.

linear_state.description: The Linear issue state after this merge report. 'Done' for shipped, 'In Progress' for reverted (needs fix), 'Todo' for blocked.

wave_progress.description: Updated wave progress after this merge.

wave_complete.description: True when all issues in the wave are now In-Production.

next_wave_ready.description: True when wave_complete is true and there is a next wave queued.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the merge report.

ota_manifest.title: OTA (Over-The-Air) Manifest
ota_manifest.description: Snapshot of the entire MAW pipeline state for a project, produced by '/tpm sync' or '/tpm status'. Used for dashboard rendering, wave advancement decisions, and audit trail. This is not a point-to-point handoff but a broadcast state document.

name.description: Project name or epic group identifier.

maw_mode.description: 'pre-real-users' auto-verifies all sizes. 'production' enforces human gates for M+.

issues.description: All active issues in the project, each annotated with pipeline state.

state.description: TPM-derived pipeline state based on Linear labels.

wave.description: Wave number this issue belongs to (1-indexed).

pr_number.description: GitHub PR number if one exists.

fix_attempts.description: Number of Builder fix iterations so far.

blocked_by.description: Issue IDs that block this issue (dependency DAG).

action_needed.description: Human-readable description of what action is needed, if any. Null when no action required.

in_progress.description: Issues actively being built, tested, or verified.

current_wave.description: The active wave number (lowest incomplete wave).

sync_actions.description: Actions taken during this sync cycle.

action.description: What the TPM did for this issue during sync.

human_attention_required.description: Issues that need human action, ordered by priority.

reason.description: Why human attention is needed, e.g. 'Verify external-system items', 'Tests-Failed 2x, auto-fix exhausted'.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the manifest generation.
